title: Conditional Drawdown at Risk (CDaR) Trade Sizer
summary: Implements the CDaR-based trade sizing algorithm from Strub (SSRN): instead of using return volatility to size positions, it applies Extreme Value Theory (EVT) to fit a generalised Pareto distribution to the tail of the historical drawdown distribution, and uses CDaR (mean of worst-alpha drawdowns) as the sizing denominator. This produces a leverage factor that is smooth, adapts to changing drawdown risk, and is more robust to fat-tailed return distributions than simple vol-scaling. The CDaR approach is distinct from all existing features because it operates on the drawdown time-series directly rather than on returns or volatility.
expected_impact: Applies Extreme Value Theory to the drawdown distribution rather than the returns distribution, computing a smooth leverage factor based on CDaR that produces 'a Sharpe ratio slightly higher than for the original strategies' and evolves smoothly to avoid high transaction costs — documented in Strub's SSRN paper across 10 years of live trading data
